tokens: Going to miss that double Ackerman (front and rear steering), I can see that now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Heading directly south right now to miss some craters off to our right - very subdued craters.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm going to take a little zigzag here...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get a feel for this thing.
(To Jim) Hold the geology; let's get the Rover squared away first.
(Pause) Okay; 8 kilometers (per hour) up a little rise.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Noticing a sharp rimmed crater) That's a nice, young, fresh one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Our TV pan suggests, you can go straight for St.
George Crater, and you'll find Elbow okay.
And we're suggesting you omit Checkpoint 1.
Rhysling Crater should be a good landmark along the way, and head 208.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Now we're heading uphill; when we head uphill, it drops down to about 8.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No dust, Joe, no dust at all.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jimmy, we're standing by for amp readouts.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, amp readout is...Looks like 15 on 1.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I guess...Could this be Rhysling right here, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Probably is...This large depression off to our left?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, I can see I'm going to have to keep my eye on the road.
(Pause) Boy, it's really rolling hills, Joe.
Oh, and this must be Earthlight, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, look at that; we're going to have to do some fancy maneuvering here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's an elongate depression here (at about 73.2 and stretching from BL.8 to BM.6) before you get to Rhysling.
I don't think we're to Rhysling yet...Rhysling ought to be about 1.4.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...we think you're short of Rhysling now.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Do you think that's probably Rhysling out about 11:00 o'clock to us, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Joe, the Rover handles quite well.
We're moving at, I guess, an average of about 8 kilometers an hour.
It's got very low damping compared to the one-g Rover, but the stability is about the same.
It negotiates small craters quite well, although there's a lot of roll.
It feels like we need the seat belts, doesn't it, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The steering is quite responsive even with only the rear steering.
There doesn't seem to be too much slip.
I can maneuver pretty well with the thing.
If I need to make a turn sharply, why, it responds quite well.
There's no accumulation of dirt in the wire wheels.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just like in the owner's manual, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, we're heading right...(Stopping to listen to Joe) Yeah, man!
Okay, I've got it on the wall here (that is, at full throttle) for a minute, and we're up to 12 (km/hr).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As soon we head upslope, it drops off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Or, are you deliberately slowing down?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I slowed down in order to get my feeling before we start sprinting.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at this little fresh one.
Lots of very angular frags all over the thing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we passed several of those.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; I'm going to cut down to the south here, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, that'd probably be best, because I think that's probably...Let's see, range (is) point 7.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we have a large subdued one at our 1 o'clock position, I'd estimate 50 kilometers (means "meters") wide.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, how we doing on the heading, Jimmer?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, if we're heading right for Elbow...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) You really have to pick your way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're only about half way to Checkpoint 1.
We shouldn't...What I thought was Rhysling was probably not Rhysling.
Rhysling is a larger crater, and it's out at about 1-point...should be about 1.4 from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) You back off on the power, it keeps right on going.
(Pause) The zero-phase lighting is pretty tough, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're going to have to make sure we keep at an angle (to downSun).
Once I look into zero-phase, it all looks flat.
(Pause) There's a nice little round one-meter crater with very angular frags all over the bottom and the rims, and glass in the very center.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, as you come up on the rille, you may want to turn your 16-millimeter camera on.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, when we get to the rille, we will, Joe.
Can't see the rille at all from here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, right now our bearing is 039 for 1.1.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Long Pause) Hey, Jim, give me...Well, I just have to drive around these craters; that's all there is to it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We have a large subdued one on our right about 60 meters wide with several small ones in the center.
By small, I mean about 10 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, it really bounces, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, I think there's sort of a...The rear end breaks out at about 10 to 12 clicks.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It sounds like it's like steering a boat, with the rear steering and the rolling motion.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, gosh, every (lost under Joe)...(Responding to Joe) Yeah, that's right.
(To Jim) Hey, here's a good fresh one right there...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I was looking at that one at 1 o'clock to us right now.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We kick up a little dust when we go through these craters.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Seems like when we get to the bottom...And I can see the trajectory of the fragments coming from the...It looks like...Yeah, they're coming from the front wheels and coming up kind of around my arm and then forward.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No...No, it looks like millimeter-type particles.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I think there's a large one coming up about 12:30 or 1 o'clock that could be Rhysling.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, that sounds good or it could be the large one to the northwest of Rhysling (at about
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, there's a large one over there, too, Joe, I...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) Man, this is really a rocking-rolling ride, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Never been on a ride like this before.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm glad they've got this great suspension system on this thing.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, here's a big one right here on our left, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, but I don't think it's big enough to be Rhysling.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I don't think it is either.
We got a ridge up here in front of us, we'll...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What did I say, Joe, about 1.6, 1.7, for Rhysling?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get on top of...(Stops to listen to Joe) That could be Rhysling, Dave; we'll find out when we get up on top of this ridge.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) By the way, Houston, your comm is crystal clear for us up here.

tokens: Maybe we ought to take this gear to Flagstaff next time.

tokens: Off in the west now, I can see
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I've seen it all the way.

tokens: Your range to Rhysling is about 1.7; so you may be short of that still.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It just clicked off 1.7, and our relative bearing's 036.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we're coming up on the right...

tokens: Hey, you can see the rille!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're looking down and across the rille, we can see craters on the far side of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Now we're getting into the blocky stuff, about 1 foot, quite angular, irregular surface.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're right at the edge of the rille, I bet you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're on the edge of the rille, you'd better believe it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See; Elbow is not as prominent as we thought, but there's a definite crater there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, look there's a big block on the edge of the rille there that must be 10 meters (across).
But, on the far side, I don't see anything that would suggest really layering.
There's a lot of debris, big angular blocks all the way down, but nothing that you'd really call exact layers.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get us back up on the ridge, it's smoother.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I think that heading was...We were on a heading that was a little too far west.
We're getting back up on the higher part of the rille rim.
At this point, I'd estimate the slope is probably - what?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, there's a definite ridge or rim that runs along the rille, maybe 70 (or) 80 meters from the inflection point that drops down into the rille, don't you think, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And, we might as well...We're heading right toward...We'll head toward the east side of Elbow.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We can see Elbow, and we can see the Front all the way down to the Spur (that is, Spur Crater).

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I see one large block, about a quarter of the way up the Front, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Chuckling) There's a big one (a rock close at hand) partially buried.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looking up at the front now, Joe, I sure see the linear patterns that Dave commented on before.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, that was a good stroke.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's a good stroke, all right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, I sure get the impression it's a...Almost looks like a slump feature, but we'll take some good pictures of that.
(Pause) You see the same linear-type pattern in the east side of the rille.
And note the linear pattern there is parallel.
Almost like layering in the rille.
And, then as you look upslope - up the Front - that layering takes that dip to the northeast that Dave had mentioned earlier.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And can you actually see the east side of the rille, towards the south there?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I can see...Looking directly south I can see the exposure that faces northwest.
I can look down and I think I can see
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I think I can see the south rim of
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It should be 2.7 (kilometers to Elbow Crater).
So we got another .7 to go.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Speed's been generally about 10 clicks (kilometers per hour).

tokens: Yeah, but it takes attention paying.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And again, looking to the south along the edge of the rille that faces to the northwest, I can see several large blocks that have rolled downslope.
Very large blocks that are about three-quarters of the way down the slope, into the rille.
That's just at the base of St.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And...Let's see, we're heading about 165 right now.
Tried to stay on the fairly level and smooth part of the Rille rim.
But looking over to the edge of the rille at this point, I see a large concentration of large boulders - large rocks.
And I'd estimate the size...They're angular, and they're all of the same color and texture as far as I can tell from here.
Well, you'd better watch the road, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I see what you're saying there, but you keep talking; let me drive.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's the first good concentration of large rocks that I've seen.
Very similar (in size and angularity) to the large rocks that 14 saw up at the top of Cone.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And your range should be coming up on 3.1, at Station 1.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, right now, Joe, our bearing is 18 and range is 2.3.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; now, Joe, I can see the bottom of the valley - Head Valley - that leads down toward
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I can see the bottom of the rille.
I see two very large boulders that are right on the surface, there (that is, they are unburied) - on the top of the very smooth portion, of the bottom of the rille.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And is the bottom V-shaped or fairly flat?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'd estimate maybe, oh, 200 meters wide of a flat area in the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, and I can see what we thought was Bridge Crater.

It's just a depression in the west wall of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And I...Boy, at this vantage point, there's sure a lot more blocks exposed on the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...on the far side of the rille.
I'm contrasting now the rille to the southeast...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Picking up his comment again)...and the side of the rille to the northwest.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Copy all that, loud and clear.
And Dave, are the front wheels wandering off of straight ahead as you drive along there?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It's just (that) there are a lot of craters and it's just sporty driving; I've just got to keep my eye on the road every second.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: By golly, it's a real test for the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...just want to get some engineering information here.
Apparently, your front wheels are tracking straight ahead, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, of course, when we turn, they dig in, and it makes the rear end break out (that is, start to swing around).
But it's okay; we can handle it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I might add to Jim's comment, that the near side of the rille wall is smooth without any outcrops, there by St.
George, and the far side has got all sorts of debris.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm sure we could drive down; I don't think we could drive back out.

tokens: Oh, now I can turn around and look to the northwest - where the rille trends to the north.

tokens: Okay, the range estimate must have been off for Elbow.

tokens: Our estimate, Jim, was 3.1 from your landing site (that is, from the presumed landing site near November Crater).

tokens: Yeah, but there's...Yeah, there's a nice fresh one there.

tokens: Yeah, but you want to go a little farther east.

tokens: Gosh, that's a long way away.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (It feels) like we've been driving for an hour.

tokens: Yeah, you want to go farther east, Dave.

tokens: Down this little crater; back up.

tokens: See, that's Elbow out at our 1 o'clock position.

tokens: The one we were just trending into.

tokens: Maybe you can, you know, angle up hill here?

tokens: (Pause) How are we doing on time there, Houston?

tokens: Continue on, and we'll give you the exact number in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Do we want to stop at Elbow, or press on?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Follow the Checklist, just as planned.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Okay, let's go right up on the ridge line there, I see some debris.

tokens: Jim, can you get an Amp reading for us as you climb?

tokens: (Garbled, probably as they hit a small crater)

tokens: Okay, now we're up on the high part, and we're on the east rim - east rim of Elbow.

tokens: Okay, this ought to give the folks back home something to look at.

tokens: And we've got a fair amount of dust on the Rover.
